pi_items.source: verifier.py checks: abs(agt_s['lat'] - gt_s['lat']) <= 0.01 and abs(agt_s['lon'] - gt_s['lon']) <= 0.01

privileged_info_summary: Verified that the data is real: the event is the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake from the USGS QuakeML catalog (id: us6000m0xl, origin: 37.4874, 137.271), and the stations are from the real GEOFON GE network provided via FDSN StationXML. Based on the XML data, the exact Haversine distances to the epicenter are: KWP (8397.6 km), GSI (5712.5 km), BKB (4775.5 km), SANI (4547.5 km), and TOLI (4381.7 km). The verifier strictly enforces a coordinate tolerance of 0.01 degrees and a distance tolerance of 5.0 km. Both output file paths explicitly match the export scripts.
